verses.5: that they may keep you from the strange woman,
verses.6: For at the window of my house,
verses.7: I saw among the simple ones.
verses.8: passing through the street near her corner,
verses.9: in the twilight, in the evening of the day,
verses.10: Behold, there a woman met him with the attire of a prostitute,
verses.12: Now she is in the streets, now in the squares,
verses.13: So she caught him, and kissed him.
verses.14: “Sacrifices of peace offerings are with me.
verses.15: Therefore I came out to meet you,
verses.16: I have spread my couch with carpets of tapestry,
verses.17: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
verses.18: Come, let’s take our fill of loving until the morning.
verses.19: For my husband isn’t at home.
verses.20: He has taken a bag of money with him.
verses.21: With persuasive words, she led him astray.
verses.23: Until an arrow strikes through his liver,
verses.24: Now therefore, sons, listen to me.
verses.25: Don’t let your heart turn to her ways.
verses.26: for she has thrown down many wounded.
verses.27: Her house is the way to Sheol,
